content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To      :   File - 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18901          Date  :   April 13, 1978

From    :   FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
            Barbara Campbell

Subject:    Proposed Response from Assemblyman Roos

        On Tuesday, April 11, 1978, at 3:25 p.m., I called
Mike Galizio, Administrative Assistant in Assemblyman
Mike Roos' District Office, concerning the proposed
response that he had submitted to us. Mr. Galizio wanted to
know if the response could be sent at public expense after
the Assemblyman filed his declaration of candidacy.

        Mr. Galizio had sent the materials to me after our
March 30, 1978, telephone conversation in which I informally
advised him that, based on his description of the materials,
their proposed response could not come within the exception
granted in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18901 (b)(1).

        I called Mr. Galizio to advise him that the proposed
response did not come within the exception granted in 2 Cal.
Adm. Code Section 18901(b)(1). This response was agreed
upon at the April 11, 1978, Opinion Request meeting. The
conclusion was based on the fact that although Assemblyman
Roos' newsletter did contain a section allowing readers to
submit their own ideas for legislation, there was nothing
in the newsletter to indicate that people who replied to
the newsletter would receive continuing response. Moreover, Assemblyman Roos had previously responded to those
people who replied to the newsletter. (See copy of the
attached November 3, 1977, letter.)

        Mr. Galizio was out of the office when I called. However, I did leave the above message with his secretary. I
told her Mr. Galizio could call if he had any questions. I
checked with that office on Wednesday, April 12, 1978.
The secretary said he had no comments or questions on the
message.

OFFICE OF
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814
(916) 445-7634

MICHAEL ROOS
ASSEMBLYMAN, 46TH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT

CRIMINAL JUSTICE
HOUSING AND
COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT

ALSO SERVES ON
LAW ENFORCEMENT
DEAD-ALZED TRAFFIC

Assembly
California Legislature

MIKE ROOS
ASSEMBLYMAN, 46TH ASSEMBLY DISTRICT

First Street, Apt

[illegible]

November 3, 1977

Dear:

I sincere thank you for responding to my newsletter.
I am happy to see that so many people are interested in local
public affairs.

If you requested particular booklets or pamphlets, you
will be receiving them shortly under separate cover.

If you offered your opinions and comments about legisla-
tion now being considered in the "Write Your Own Bill" section,
please be assured I will read and respond to your questions,
comments and ideas.

If I can be of any further service, please feel free to
call. Again, thank you for your interest. Please be patient
for our package or letter.

Sincerely,

MIKE ROOS
46th Assembly District

MR:br

Dear Friend:

Thank you for your patience in awaiting the questionaire results.
The tremendous response kept my office answering many requests and
recording all the enlightening suggestions and opinions. As one
of the many concerned citizens who took the time to respond, I am
happy to share those ideas with you today. I would also like to
reassure you that we are still working to fill all the pamphlet
orders, and to resolve the many special problems that were brought
to my attention.

Over 1200 people replied to the questionaire, 450 of whom used the
"write your own bill" section to bring their views and opinions to
my attention. More than 700 issues were touched on by these sugges-
tions, many of which were related to the following areas of concern:

ISSUE                                          NO. OF RESPONSES

Rental Rates                                    74
Auto Insurance Rates                            68
Property Tax Reform                             69
Tax Reform (Other)                              50
Rent Control                                    54
Pornography                                     38
Senior Citizen Problems                         39
Crime and Sentance Reform                       34
Right to Sexual Preference                      24
Lower Government Spending                       21
Illegal Aliens                                  17
Community Clean-up and Conservation             25

Many of these issues have been addressed by recent proposals and
legislation in the Assembly which will give me the opportunity to
work toward effective changes in these areas.

On the community level, I was encouraged by the interest on the
part of so many citizens in working together to solve unsafe conditions
and to help each other through neighborhood protection programs.
Currently we are working to get action taken on many unsafe conditions
reported to our office. I hope you continue to bring these conditions
to my attention; together we can make city officials aware of the
specific areas.

My office is always willing to listen and available to help alleviate
these problems. I hope you continue to seek my assistance in any
difficulty you face. I will do my best to help.

Again, I would like to thank you for taking the time to respond.
I appreciate knowing your opinions on these and all the subjects that
concern you as a member of the 46th district. Please continue to
make your views known to me.

Sincerely,

Free Booklets from Assemblyman Roos

For your information and convenience, Mike has the following booklets and pamphlets available. You may obtain free copies by placing a check mark next to the title you would like sent.

____ CALIFORNIA STATE ASSEMBLY: An illustrated book-
let on the workings and organization of the State
Assembly.

____ THE CONSUMER AND THE SMALL CLAIMS COURT:
An explanation of the uses and procedures by the
consumer of this judicial process. Available in En-
glish or Spanish.
(Please designate) English ____ Spanish ____.

____ CALIFORNIA DRIVER'S HANDBOOK: A summary of
important rules for driving on California's highways.
Available in English or Spanish.
(Please designate) English ____ Spanish ____.

____ LANDLORD TENANT: Ten of the Most Frequently
Asked Questions. Available in English or Spanish.
(Please designate) English ____ Spanish ____.

____ DRUGS: Buying and using prescription and over-
the-counter drugs.

____ UNDERSTANDING YOUR UTILITY BILL: How to read
your meter and estimate the bill.

____ SAVING ENERGY AT HOME, IT'S YOUR MONEY:
Helpful hints to reduce energy requirements in your
home. Available in English or Spanish.
(Please designate) English ____ Spanish ____.

____ CONSTITUTION OF THE UNITED STATES AND THE
STATE OF CALIFORNIA: Includes a constitutional
history of California and many historical docu-
ments.

____ INSURANCE INFORMATION FOR CALIFORNIANS:
Information regarding problems, requests and
complaints about your insurance.

____ ESTIMATING YOUR SOCIAL SECURITY RETIREMENT
CHECK: Information about social security retire-
ment benefits.

____ CREDIT FOR WOMEN: New laws regarding a
woman's credit standing.

____ CONSUMER INFORMATION CATALOGUE: A history
and explanation of consumer pamphlets and
books.

(Limited quantities available on some of the above items; these booklets are not available to groups unless by
special request)


return address                                                                                                                                           stamp
_________________________________
_________________________________
_________________________________


Mike Roos
Assemblyman – 46th District
600 South New Hampshire – 3rd Floor
Los Angeles, CA 90005

The Senior Citizen's Page

Discounts save money!! Below are listed many free and discounted services available to seniors through specified public service programs. Assemblyman Roos urges your review and participation in many of these unique programs:

Telephone

Low-cost telephone service is available for those who request it. Pacific and General Telephone Companies offer this service in place of the regulator service. It can save you money if you do not make many local calls each month.

Utilities

All seniors age 62 and over whose gross income is $7500 a year or less are entitled to big savings on their utility bills under two new programs:

Utility Users' Tax Exemption: Eliminates the 5% City tax from telephone, electric, water and gas bills.

Lifeline: Provides a 50% discount on a large part of the water and power bill (first 360 kwh of electricity and first 1800 cubic feet of water on each bill).

Simple applications and eligibility information are available at any city library, police station, social security office, DWP office or contact Assemblyman Roos' office.

Senior Safety Bill
With Assemblyman Mike Roos
(D-Los Angeles) as the swing vote, SB 370, designed to protect senior citizens from violent crimes, passed out of the Assembly Criminal Justice Committee.

"I couldn't sit by and allow this powerful legal tool to lie unused in light of the rising crime rate against senior citizens," Roos commented.

The bill authored by George Deukmejian (R-Long Beach) would greatly prohibit anyone who commits or attempts to commit nine categories of crime, including murder, assault, robbery, kidnap, first degree burglary, or rape and inflicts great bodily injury to the victim.

Roos stated, "Specifically, this bill is aimed at perpetrators of crimes against people 65 years of age or older, blind people, paraplegics or quadraplegics," Roos continued. "SB 370 has been languishing in committee for three years. This bill is far overdue. It is past time to crack down on criminals who prey on the elderly and physically handicapped."

Food

Here are some helpful suggestions to fight the high cost of food.

Good Stamps

Food stamps can be an important supplement to your income. To find out if you qualify, call the Office of Public Inquiry at 572-5863.

Food Cooperatives

"Food Buying Clubs" provide good food at cheap prices – 30-50% off and more savings. For information contact the City's Senior Line at 488-1133.

Drugs

Drugs and medicines can cost less if you ask your doctor to prescribe them by their generic, or general name, instead of their brand name. For example, there are many brands of aspirin. Some cost more than others, but aspirin is aspirin! Federal law says the ingredients cannot be different.

Have your doctor write prescriptions using generic names for your drugs and medicines. It will save you money!

DISTRICT OFFICE ADDRESS
600 S. NEW HAMPSHIRE AVE.
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90005
(213) 386-8042

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 445-7644

COMMITTEES
Criminal Justice
Housing
Community Development

SUBCOMMITTEE
Law Enforcement and
Specialized Training

Assembly
California Legislature

MIKE ROOS
ASSEMBLYMAN, FORTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

Dear Neighbor:

I am so very delighted to greet you in this way as your new
Assemblyman. My family and I worked very hard for the responsi-
bility you have entrusted to me. Therefore, this will be only
one of a variety of ways I'll seek to strengthen and improve
upon our relationship.

As you may know, it is my job to evaluate and help enact
responsible laws for the state. It is hard to imagine, but some-
where between 1,000 and 1,500 bills pass the Legislature each
year. Some are new laws. Most are changes in existing law.

These laws affect the way you are taxed, how much you are
taxed, what kinds of penalties we have for criminals, what is
taught in school, where the next freeway is built, where the next
park is located, how much should be spent on health care and who
should qualify for welfare.

The stakes are high for all of us. I have the training to
do a good job in your behalf, but that is really only a part of
the challenge.

My wife, Ann Marie and I walked door-to-door in the campaign.
We stressed your involvement in state affairs. It is vitally
important to me not only to have your thinking, but also your
participation.

This first Newsletter is a challenge to you to get involved
Your participation with your neighbors in your community and with
your governmental representatives at all levels will result not
only in lowering the costs of government that have gotten out of
hand, but also increase the safety and sense of reliance we seek
from each other.

I urge you to contact me at your convenience.

Sincerely,

MIKE ROOS
46th Assembly District

State of California

Memorandum

To      :   Opinion Request Meeting Participants                    Date   :April 7, 1978




From  :  FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
          Barbara Campbell

Subject:  Letter from Assemblyman Roos, November 3, 1977.


          Can the proposed response be sent at public expense under
          the exemption in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18901(b)(1)?

          The initial newsletter will be available at the meeting.


          BC:jo

STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814
(916) 445-7534

DISTRICT OFFICES
6530 W. SUNSET BLVD
LOS ANGELES CA 90028
(213) 466-5842

Assembly
California Legislature

COMMITTEE:
CRIMINAL JUSTICE
HOUSING AND
COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT
SUB-COMMITTEE
LAW ENFORCEMENT
SPECIALIZED TRAINING

MIKE ROOS
ASSEMBLYMAN, FORTY-SIXTH DISTRICT

First letter sent
immediately about
receipt & exten
possible requests
of white gown &
bill questionnaire
response -

November 3, 1977

Dear:

We sincerely thank you for responding to my newsletter.
I am happy to see that so many people are interested in local
public affairs.

If you requested particular booklets or pamphlets, you
will be receiving them shortly under separate cover.

If you offered your opinions and comments about legisla-
tion or local problems in the "Write Your Own Bill" section,
please be assured I will read and respond to your questions,
comments and ideas.

If I can be of any further service, please feel free to
call. Again, thank you for your interest. Please be patient
for our package or letter.

Sincerely,

MIKE ROOS
46th Assembly District

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Assemblyman Roos' proposed response to constituent replies regarding his newsletter questionnaire can be sent at public expense under the specified regulatory exemption.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the proposed response does not qualify for the public expense exemption because the newsletter did not establish an expectation of continuing responses, and the Assemblyman had already provided previous responses to questionnaire replies.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: OCR document with some illegible sections. Clear question and conclusion sections present. Document includes the actual response letter dated November 3, 1977 from Assemblyman Roos and materials related to an April 11, 1978 Opinion Request meeting.

Mr. Galizio wanted to know if the response could be sent at public expense after the Assemblyman filed his declaration of candidacy. Mr. Galizio had sent the materials to me after our March 30, 1978, telephone conversation in which I informally advised him that, based on his description of the materials, their proposed response could not come within the exception granted in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18901 (b)(1). I called Mr. Galizio to advise him that the proposed response did not come within the exception granted in 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18901(b)(1). This response was agreed upon at the April 11, 1978, Opinion Request meeting. The conclusion was based on the fact that although Assemblyman Roos' newsletter did contain a section allowing readers to submit their own ideas for legislation, there was nothing in the newsletter to indicate that people who replied to the newsletter would receive continuing response. Moreover, Assemblyman Roos had previously responded to those people who replied to the newsletter. (See copy of the attached November 3, 1977, letter.) Mr. Galizio was out of the office when I called. However, I did leave the above message with his secretary. I told her Mr. Galizio could call if he had any questions. I checked with that office on Wednesday, April 12, 1978. The secretary said he had no comments or questions on the message. embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter addressing whether Assemblyman Mike Roos could send a proposed response to constituent questionnaire replies at public expense. The Commission determined the response did not qualify for the exemption under 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18901(b)(1) due to the lack of indication of continuing responses and prior correspondence with respondents.
